mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales (RSAFS) indicate robust consumer spending and aggregate demand, which boosts corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC of RSAFS level or 3-month change vs 20-day forward returns is below 0.03 over a 3-year rolling window, or average forward return in top quintile of RSAFS growth is not significantly positive.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) below 6.5% signals significant labor market tightening, which historically has led to further equity gains as the Federal Reserve maintains accommodative policy until full employment is reached.
failure_condition: When UNRATE is below 6.5%, the proportion of positive 5-day forward S&P 500 returns does not exceed 55%, or the average excess return over the full-sample mean is non-positive.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in industrial production (INDPRO) combined with rising housing starts (HOUST) indicates synchronized expansion in manufacturing and construction, signalling a durable economic upswing that favours equities over a quarter.
failure_condition: The interaction term (INDPRO change × HOUST change) in a regression predicting 60-day forward returns has a t-statistic < 1.6 or the partial correlation with returns is not positive.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) is at or near the zero lower bound, further monetary easing is not possible via rate cuts, so equity markets respond positively to confirmations that the Fed will sustain low rates until the output gap closes, reinforced by low inflation (CPIAUCSL).
failure_condition: During periods with FEDFUNDS at 0.25% or lower, 20-day forward S&P 500 returns are not significantly positive (one-sided t-test p > 0.10), or the joint signal with CPIAUCSL yields no predictive improvement over using FEDFUNDS alone.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation (CPIAUCSL) erodes real corporate earnings and increases the risk of future monetary tightening, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over a one-month horizon when the index level is rising.
failure_condition: The correlation between month-over-month CPIAUCSL change and 20-day forward returns is non-negative, or the average forward return in months with above-median inflation change is not below the full-sample mean.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) rising above the 1,000,000-unit annualized pace signals a self-sustaining housing recovery, which historically has led to positive short-term equity returns as construction activity boosts employment and related spending.
failure_condition: When HOUST exceeds 1000, the proportion of positive 5-day forward returns is at or below 50%, or the average 5-day return is not statistically greater than zero.

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth combined with accelerating GDP (GDPC1) confirms that labour market gains translate into broad economic momentum, driving positive S&P 500 returns over a quarter as the expansion gains traction.
failure_condition: The joint signal from PAYEMS and GDPC1 (e.g., product of z-scores) fails to produce a positive average forward return in the top quartile of signal strength, or the interaction term in a predictive regression is not significant at the 10% level.

mechanism_narrative: In the post-recession recovery regime, growth in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects improving capacity utilization and corporate confidence, which historically leads to positive S&P 500 returns over a one-month horizon, whereas in a late-cycle regime the same growth signal would be weaker or negative.
failure_condition: Within the post-recession window (2010-01 to 2013-04), the rank IC of INDPRO growth on 20-day forward returns is not positive, or the average forward return in months with above-median INDPRO growth is not greater than in below-median months.
